__description__: Metadata registry for all protocol documents (repo + external)

content.question: How should the organism store and consume artifacts across sessions?
content.hypothesis: Deterministic schema (maturity, consumer, lifecycle) reduces metabolic waste (re-parsing)
content.method: Designed from K15 law + empirical observation of artifact accumulation
content.result: 7-section protocol with maturity model, consumer registry, cleanup cadence

falsification.success_criterion: All falsification gates execute correctly; no artifact re-parsing; consumers activate as planned
falsification.failure_criterion: Any gate fails; session context time > 30s; artifacts re-parsed > 2x per session
falsification.what_fails_hypothesis: If gates fail, protocol is incomplete or assumptions are wrong → redesign for v1.1

auto_delete_if.reason: Protocol v2.0 complete (complete redesign); v1.0 superseded
auto_delete_if.action: Archive ORGANISM_PROTOCOL v1.0 to dead/archive/, commit v2.0

consumer_registry.action: Load consumer_registry.json, report WAITING consumers and blockers

references.notes: SSOT for protocol design + implementation status. Versioning via protocol_version.json in external storage.

content.question: What is the formal specification for organism artifacts?
content.method: 7-section detailed design covering schema, storage, consumers, lifecycle, session injection, debt, versioning

content.question: What is the current state of organism artifacts?
content.result: 30+ orphaned artifacts, 9 VALIDATED with zero consumers, R5 violations identified

lifecycle.reason: Purpose complete (audit done, artifacts migrated, protocol implemented)

auto_delete_if.reason: Transient analysis document, no permanent value after implementation
auto_delete_if.date_threshold: 2026-05-07 (one week after creation, after Phase 2 cleanup)
auto_delete_if.action: Delete from repo, keep in git history

artifacts.notes: Can be referenced if audit questions arise, but protocol is now live and this doc is superseded by actual implementation.

content.question: What was done to implement the protocol?
content.method: Logged each infrastructure, migration, wiring step with cost-benefit
content.result: Phase 1 infrastructure complete, 8 artifacts migrated, 4 consumers registered

lifecycle.reason: Historical record of implementation, never deleted

consumer_registry.action: Reference for understanding what was implemented and cost-benefit

content.question: What work was completed in this session?
content.method: Summarized scope + execution + blockers + next steps
content.result: Phase 1 complete, protocol live, ready for Phase 2 gate

lifecycle.reason: Historical record of session work, never deleted

consumer_registry.action: Reference for tracking session progress and handoff

session_init.description: Loads ORGANISM_PROTOCOL.md at session start, reports blockers

protocol_auditor.description: Verifies all artifacts meet protocol compliance
protocol_auditor.action: Check for orphaned artifacts, missing metadata, violated cleanup cadence

session_history.description: Records of what was done and why

cleanup_schedule.2026-05-07: Delete ARTIFACT_AUDIT_2026_05_03.md (one week after creation, purpose complete)
cleanup_schedule.phase2_completion: Validate all VALIDATED consumers are wired and active
cleanup_schedule.phase3_completion: Validate all phase 3 gates executed correctly; confirm protocol hypothesis
cleanup_schedule.2026-12-31: Archive ORGANISM_PROTOCOL.md v1.0 if v2.0 exists; keep historical copy

falsification.protocol_hypothesis: Mechanized artifact lifecycle reduces session metabolic cost while maintaining correctness
falsification.success_criterion: Session context time < 10s AND zero artifact re-parsing AND Phase 2/3 gates execute correctly
falsification.failure_criterion: Context time > 30s OR artifacts re-parsed > 2x per session OR gates fail > 20% of time
